key_highlights: Geopolitical tensions dominate: US-Iran war continues, with Iran's peace proposal rejected by Trump, leading to oil price surge and supply disruption risks.
Trump-Xi high-stakes meeting in Beijing on Thursday adds uncertainty to global trade and currency markets.
US jobs data shows 115,000 new jobs in April, below expectations, reflecting Iran war impact on economy.
Inflation drumbeat persists, with consumers unnerved and Fed rate hike risk flagged by PIMCO CIO.
AI sector remains a bright spot: Cerebras IPO price range raised to $150-$160 due to surging demand; Alphabet poised to become world's biggest company on AI wins.
Oil market runs down safety cushion as supply shock worsens; Qatar sends first LNG shipment through Hormuz since war started.
Mortgage rates move back up, with 30- and 15-year rates rising, impacting housing market.
Trump Media's Q1 loss widens to $406 million on bitcoin and CRO markdowns.
Oklo stock receives multiple bullish calls (Tigress Financial initiates coverage, potential 10X returns touted).
Spice company wins suit over 10% Trump global tariffs, signaling legal challenges to trade policy.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surge as Iran peace deal hopes fade; supply disruption worsens with Hormuz shipping risks and new Russian-flagged LNG tanker loading sanctioned gas. Aramco CEO warns of long disruption.
sectors.implications: Inflationary pressure intensifies, benefiting oil producers but hurting consumers and transport sectors. NACHO trade (betting on higher oil and inflation) gains traction.

sectors.trend: AI sector remains strong: Cerebras IPO demand surges, Alphabet poised to become world's biggest company on AI wins, Scribe raises $1.3B for AI employee recording software. AI shock compared to China shock by top economist.
sectors.implications: AI stocks continue to attract capital; investors should focus on companies with clear AI monetization. Nvidia remains tough to beat per Jim Cramer.

sectors.trend: Wells Fargo revamps CoreWeave stock price target; Binance fills banking gaps in emerging markets. Fed rate hike risk due to Iran war could pressure bank margins.
sectors.implications: Banks may benefit from higher rates but face credit risk from economic slowdown. Fintech and crypto platforms gain traction in underserved markets.

sectors.trend: Inflation drumbeat persists, unnerving consumers. Grocery stores see surge in sparkling waters (consumer shift to cheaper options). Mortgage rates rise, housing affordability worsens.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary spending likely to slow; defensive consumer staples and discount retailers may outperform. Home equity rates tie 2026-low but equity-rich homeowners fall.

sectors.trend: Major US health provider drops prior authorization for 30% of services, praised by 93% of doctors. Hinge Health Q1 earnings call highlights.
sectors.implications: Positive for healthcare access and patient satisfaction; may reduce administrative burden and costs.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates move back up; number of 'equity-rich' homeowners falls. Camden Property Trust sees strong recovery. HELOC rates tie 2026-low.
sectors.implications: Housing market faces headwinds from higher rates, but multifamily REITs show recovery signs. Home equity extraction may slow.

sectors.trend: Troubled convenience store chain files Chapter 11; Spirit Airlines gets viral revival plan. Cruise ships face norovirus and hantavirus outbreaks. Travel insurance demand rises.
sectors.implications: Travel sector faces operational and health risks; airlines may see consolidation or restructuring. Travel insurance becomes essential.

sectors.trend: US-Iran tensions escalate; UK and France host multinational meeting on escorting ships through Hormuz. Drone hits ship near Qatar. Trump-Xi meeting adds trade uncertainty.
sectors.implications: Defense stocks may benefit from increased military spending. Supply chain disruptions for energy and shipping.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk premium: US-Iran war and Trump-Xi meeting dominate market sentiment.
Inflation persistence: Oil price surge and supply disruptions keep inflation elevated, risking Fed rate hikes.
AI investment boom: Cerebras IPO, Alphabet's AI wins, and Scribe's funding highlight AI as a growth engine.
Energy supply shock: Hormuz shipping risks and Russian LNG sanctions tighten global energy markets.
Consumer strain: Rising mortgage rates, inflation, and job market softening pressure households.
Trade policy uncertainty: Trump tariffs ruled unlawful in one case, but global trade tensions remain high.
Crypto and fintech evolution: Binance fills banking gaps; Circle reports earnings; XRP tests resistance.
Health and safety risks: Cruise ship outbreaks and drone strikes highlight operational risks in travel and shipping.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks (XOM, CVX) and defense (LMT, NOC); reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines. Consider hedging with gold (GLD) or oil futures.
Diversify away from China-exposed sectors (semiconductors, industrials). Favor domestic-focused companies and those with supply chain flexibility. Monitor ringgit and Asian currencies.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed rate hike due to inflation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate debt and short-term Treasuries. Consider TIPS for inflation protection. Financials may benefit from higher rates.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil supply disruption and price spike
risk_assessment.mitigation: Overweight energy sector; underweight airlines and transport. Use NACHO trade (long oil, short consumer). Consider renewable energy stocks as long-term hedge.
Shift from discretionary to defensive consumer staples (PG, KO) and discount retailers (WMT, COST). Monitor job market and mortgage rates.
Take profits on high-flying AI stocks with no earnings; focus on companies with strong fundamentals (NVDA, GOOGL, MSFT). Diversify into other tech subsectors.
Limit crypto exposure to 1-2% of portfolio; use stop-losses. XRP resistance at $1.50 is key; break above could signal rally, failure could lead to sharp decline.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Health and safety disruptions in travel
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid cruise line stocks (CCL, NCLH) until outbreak situation stabilizes. Travel insurance providers (TRV) may benefit.

opportunities.rationale: Oil supply disruption and Iran tensions support higher prices. Aramco CEO warns of long disruption. NACHO trade gaining traction.
AI investment boom continues with Cerebras IPO, Alphabet's AI wins, and Nvidia's dominance. AI shock is positive for productivity.
Jim Cramer has blunt message on gold; geopolitical tensions and inflation support gold prices. Central bank buying continues.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in renewable energy for long-term
opportunities.rationale: Green energy stocks are unstoppable per analysis; oil supply shock accelerates transition. Government support and Earth Day focus.
Inflation and consumer strain favor companies with pricing power and essential products. Grocery stores see shift to cheaper options.
Multiple bullish calls (Tigress Financial initiates, potential 10X returns). Nuclear energy play aligns with AI data center power demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation, rising rates, and travel disruptions (cruise outbreaks, drone strikes) pressure these sectors. Spirit Airlines revival plan is speculative.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spike with NACHO trade
defensive_moves.rationale: Wall Street is betting on higher oil and persistent inflation. Long oil futures/short consumer discretionary ETFs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation or short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty and potential Fed rate hike warrant caution. High-yield savings accounts offer up to 4.1% APY, CDs up to 4%.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid Trump Media (DJT) due to widening losses
defensive_moves.rationale: Q1 loss widened to $406 million on bitcoin and CRO markdowns. High volatility and political risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor XRP for potential breakout or fakeout
defensive_moves.rationale: XRP tests $1.50 resistance again. Break above could lead to rally, failure could trigger sharp decline. Use stop-losses.
Trump-Xi meeting outcome uncertain; ringgit likely to trade between RM3.90-3.95. Favor US and India markets.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets remain highly volatile due to US-Iran war escalation and Trump-Xi trade talks. Oil prices likely to stay elevated above $90/bbl, pressuring inflation and consumer spending. Fed rate hike risk increases. AI stocks may continue to rally but face profit-taking. Defensive positioning recommended with overweight in energy, gold, and consumer staples. US stock futures slip as Iran deal hope fades.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Resolution of geopolitical tensions (Iran truce, US-China trade deal) could trigger a sharp rally in risk assets. AI and renewable energy remain structural growth themes. Oil supply disruption may accelerate energy transition. Inflation may moderate if supply chains normalize. Fed may cut rates if economy slows. Long-term investors should accumulate quality AI and energy stocks on dips.
key_catalysts: Trump-Xi meeting in Beijing on Thursday (May 14) - outcome on trade and tariffs.
Iran peace proposal response - Trump calls it 'totally unacceptable', but further negotiations possible.
Fed meeting minutes and Powell's comments on inflation and rate path.
Cerebras IPO pricing and debut - demand surge indicates strong AI appetite.
Q4 earnings results from Bharti Airtel, HAL, and other Indian companies.
Oil inventory data and Hormuz shipping disruptions.
US consumer sentiment and inflation data (CPI/PPI) for April.
Circle (stablecoin firm) earnings report on Monday - AI focus.
UK-France multinational meeting on escorting ships through Hormuz.
Supreme Court voting rights decision and potential redistricting war.
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices - key indicator of supply disruption.
US 10-year Treasury yield - reflects inflation and rate expectations.
Dollar index (DXY) - impacted by trade talks and Fed policy.
XRP price action at $1.50 resistance - crypto market sentiment.
Malaysian ringgit (MYR) - proxy for Asia trade tensions.
Mortgage rates (30-year fixed) - housing market health.
AI-related IPOs (Cerebras, others) - investor appetite for tech.
Cruise line stocks (CCL, NCLH) - health risk impact.
Trump Media (DJT) - political and financial risk.
Oklo (OKLO) - nuclear energy and AI data center play.
NACHO trade performance - oil vs consumer discretionary.
